codeReview.summary: Re-reviewed the current working-tree patch in tests/test_concurrent_caching.py against the prior blocking finding. The thread-ID proof was replaced with a deterministic overlap assertion (`max_active > 1`) while each mocked scrape holds briefly, so strictly sequential execution cannot satisfy the assertion while concurrent execution can. This removes the prior reliance on unique-thread scheduling behavior and resolves the original sequential-pass risk.

summary: PASS: The prior blocking issue is resolved by the overlap-based assertion in test_permit_scrape_runs_in_parallel_proven_by_thread_ids; concurrency evidence now deterministically fails under sequential scraping without narrow timing thresholds.
